evals.prompt: Extract the text from this receipt image: receipt.jpg
evals.expected_output: Standardized JSON with `text` containing the OCR'd receipt content (tables/columns preserved as markdown), `summary` generated from the ocr.md prompt using a vision-capable model, and `metadata` with `source_type: "image"`, the original filename, and a `summarizer` block recording which model was picked.
assertions: local-vision MCP tool is called for OCR extraction
Tables and columns in the receipt are preserved as markdown in the text field
scripts/pick_summarizer.py is invoked with content_type 'image'
The picked model has vision: true (verified via the recommend_model response or the static fallback)
prompts/ocr.md is loaded and used for summarization
Output is a JSON object with text, summary, and metadata fields
metadata.source_type is "image"
metadata.summarizer.source is one of "llm-info", "fallback", or "hardcoded"

evals.prompt: Summarize this article for me: https://example.com/blog/ai-trends-2025
evals.expected_output: Standardized JSON with `text` containing the extracted article content, `summary` structured with Key Points and Main Takeaway sections (from url.md prompt), and `metadata` reflecting the URL as source with `source_type: "url"`.
assertions: crawling_exa is called with the provided URL
scripts/pick_summarizer.py is invoked with content_type 'url'
prompts/url.md is loaded and used for summarization
The summary includes Key Points and Main Takeaway sections
metadata.source_type is "url"
metadata includes the original URL as the filename or source identifier

evals.expected_output: Standardized JSON with `text` containing the full transcript (with timestamps if present), `summary` generated from the transcript.md prompt, and `metadata` with `source_type: "audio"` and the original filename.
assertions: faster-whisper is used for transcription with model large-v3 at /Users/cam/.cache/whisper/large-v3.pt
scripts/pick_summarizer.py is invoked with content_type 'audio'
prompts/transcript.md is loaded and used for summarization
Timestamps are included in the text field if present in the transcript
metadata.source_type is "audio"
Output is a JSON object with text, summary, and metadata fields

evals.prompt: What does this research paper cover? research_paper.pdf
evals.expected_output: Standardized JSON with `text` containing the extracted PDF content, `summary` structured with Overview and Key Sections (from document.md prompt), and `metadata` with `source_type: "pdf"` and the original filename. Because the PDF is >50KB, the picker should request use_case 'analysis'.
assertions: markitdown is used to extract the PDF content to markdown, then Claude analyzes the output
scripts/pick_summarizer.py is invoked with content_type 'pdf' and a non-zero --size-bytes value
If size > 50KB the use_case passed to recommend_model is 'analysis', not 'chat'
prompts/document.md is loaded and used for summarization
The summary includes Overview and Key Sections
metadata.source_type is "pdf"
Output is a JSON object with text, summary, and metadata fields

evals.prompt: Process these three files: slides.pptx, photo.png, unknown.xyz
evals.expected_output: Three independent result objects: slides.pptx returns complete JSON using markitdown extraction then Claude analysis, photo.png returns complete JSON using local-vision MCP, and unknown.xyz returns an error object `{"error": "...", "metadata": {...}}` without stopping the batch.
assertions: Each file is processed independently, not as a combined blob
slides.pptx uses markitdown for extraction then Claude analysis and returns a complete text/summary/metadata result
photo.png uses local-vision MCP for OCR and returns a complete text/summary/metadata result
The vision pick for photo.png is reused from cache and not re-fetched for slides.pptx
unknown.xyz returns an error object with an error message and metadata but does not stop batch processing
The two valid files (slides.pptx and photo.png) return complete results despite the error on unknown.xyz

evals.prompt: Extract text from screenshot.png while the mcpcentral-llm-info server is unreachable.
evals.expected_output: OCR completes via local-vision; pick_summarizer.py falls back to the static map (qwen2.5-vl:latest for image), sets metadata.summarizer.source to "fallback", and returns a valid result object.
assertions: scripts/pick_summarizer.py runs even when mcpcentral-llm-info returns no response
The static fallback maps image → qwen2.5-vl:latest
metadata.summarizer.source is "fallback"
The skill does not error or refuse to process when the registry is unreachable
